title: AI fortifies liberty, prosperity, rule of law
primary_topics: AI as instrument of liberty and prosperity
sub_topics: generative AI capacity to curate data and create content
First Lady Louise Araneta Marcos attendance
Rachel Lois B. Gella's prize-winning dissertation
University of St. La Salle Bacolod
Justice George Malcolm on liberty beyond physical restraint
International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights
SC Strategic Plan for Judicial Innovation 2022-2027

stances.claim: Lawyers and the public should not fear AI disruption — humans will not be replaced by AI per se, but can be replaced by a person using AI; the key is liberty of thought combined with AI's algorithmic power.
stances.rhetorical_move: endorsement of MVP's framing + complement-not-replace doctrine

stances.claim: AI cannot run the world by itself — it must interact with human beings; humans retain irreplaceable touch, empathy, instincts, ability to connect, and capacity to navigate emotions of crises.

There can be no real liberty in the digital world when consent is rendered illusory — commercial practices that influence users against their actual interests compromise digital consent, paraphrasing Justice Malcolm's principle that liberty cannot be dwarfed into mere freedom from physical restraint.
stances.rhetorical_move: transmission of Gella dissertation's argument as authoritative + Malcolm citation

stances.claim: The 1987 Constitution, the ICCPR, the Civil Code, and the Data Privacy Act of 2012 collectively protect digital consent — the legal infrastructure exists, but enforcement must be active.

Properly contextualized and used, AI can fortify the safeguarding of liberty and the nurturing of prosperity under the rule of law — the Supreme Court's Strategic Plan for Judicial Innovation 2022-2027 and Congress's AI bills (HB 7396, HB 7913) are correct institutional responses.
stances.rhetorical_move: synthesis of his core philosophical formula with new technological reality

institutions: University of St. La Salle Bacolod
Department of the Interior and Local Government
laws_treaties: International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights
events: Promulgation of SC Strategic Plan for Judicial Innovation 2022-2027

decision_framework_signals: applies twin-beacons framework to AI — AI must serve liberty and prosperity, not displace them
uses human-AI complementarity (not substitution) lens to address technological-displacement fears
frames digital consent as the new frontier of liberty (Malcolm principle extended to digital age)
synthesizes legal-infrastructure inventory (Constitution + ICCPR + Civil Code + Data Privacy Act + new bills) as the operational protection regime
notable_anecdotes: MVP's deadpan joke that lawyers should fear AI only because 'it will drastically reduce their billable hours' — eliciting nod and smile from First Lady Louise Araneta Marcos
